description_long: This is a small, decorative pagoda structure featuring a single-tiered, blue-tiled roof with slightly upturned eaves, supported by robust wooden beams. The roof is capped with a simple, rectangular blue finial, and the wooden framework is reinforced with blue brackets at each joint. The central body of the pagoda is constructed from textured stone blocks, giving it a sturdy and traditional appearance. The upper section includes a wooden balustrade, adding ornamental detail and a sense of openness. The structure is compact and vertical, with no visible doors or windows, suggesting it serves a decorative or symbolic purpose rather than as a functional building. The overall design is reminiscent of East Asian architectural motifs, particularly those found in Japanese or Chinese garden pagodas.
description: A compact, single-tiered pagoda with a blue-tiled roof, wooden frame, and stone core, featuring upturned eaves and ornamental details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the blue-tiled roof, wooden beams, and central stone section with a clear view of the balustrade.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the roof, the wooden support structure, and a protruding wooden element on the right.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full wooden framework, stone core, and the absence of a balustrade, with more visible support beams.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the wooden frame, blue roof, and a protruding wooden element on the left.
